Articles

Get started

New here? Read these first — the data-addressing model and your role’s path, then how to connect to the services erifunctions talks to.

Getting started with erifunctions
Connecting to Azure, ODK Central, SharePoint, and Teams

Quick reference & onboarding

Desk references and the new-analyst path: the big-picture orientation, the paced onboarding track, the one-page cheat sheet, the channel-vs-measure data-model card, and the troubleshooting card.

Orientation: the data system at a glance
Onboarding a new Data Analyst
Data Analyst cheat sheet
Data-model decision card: channel vs. measure
Troubleshooting card

For data analysts

The daily DA workflow, in order: stand up a space, bring data through the raw → staged → approved gate, then keep the backlog clean. CMR and ODK as those feeds arrive.

Onboarding a new country, disease, or data type (for data analysts)
Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
Answering ad-hoc data requests with SQL (eri_query)
Branded tables, figures, and decks for outputs
Quality-check an extract and give a country feedback
Final summaries and reports from an ODK survey

For epidemiologists

Source approved data, reconcile free-text places to admin units, quality-check an extract for epidemiological sense, and compute programme indicators.

A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
Reconciling free-text localities to admin units (for epidemiologists)
Catching anomalies in a new surveillance extract (for epidemiologists)
Epidemiological Analytics

Topic deep-dives

How the engines work, beyond the role walkthroughs: the data-quality pipeline, spatial sourcing and joins, and sharing via SharePoint and Teams.

Data Quality Pipeline
Spatial Workflow
SharePoint Workflow

Extending the package

Add a new country, disease, or programme — author its schema and disease analytics, and contribute them to the package.

Adding a New NTD Program
